trades.sentiment_summary: Instacart (CART) reported strong Q4 CY2025 results today, with revenue of $992 million beating estimates by 2% and adjusted EPS of $0.97 exceeding expectations by 2.8%, driving the stock up roughly 15% to around $38.[2][3] Analysts responded positively, with Needham raising its price target to $55 (Buy) and Benchmark to $55 (Buy), amid highlights of accelerating multi-engine growth and expanding grocery tech partnerships.[1][3][4][5] The stock gapped up on heavy volume following these upgrades, though consensus remains Hold with a $51.54 average target.[3][4]

Scotiabank raised its FY2027 EPS estimates for Alamos Gold (AGI) from $4.11 to $4.13, signaling positive analyst outlook[1]. Van Eck Associates reported a significant 9.96% passive stake in AGI as of December 31, 2025, indicating strong institutional confidence[3]. No other major news events for AGI appeared in the last 24 hours, with one unrelated mention of a Brazilian fintech IPO under a different ticker[2].

Core & Main closed its acquisition of Pioneer Supply, adding locations in Oklahoma and Texas to expand its waterworks distribution network. The stock is trading steadily around $57.63-$57.84 with minimal change. Analyst consensus remains Moderate Buy with targets up to $65, supported by growth catalysts like infrastructure spending and projected EPS increases.

CommScope Holding Company (COMM) stock rose 3.89% to 18.50 in recent trading, indicating positive price momentum. Analyst consensus from three analysts rates COMM as a Buy, with a 2026 price target of $16, though 67% recommend holding. No specific negative news or events directly impacted COMM in the last 24 hours amid broader market declines in communication services.

Public Sector Pension Investment Board bought 15,610 shares of Amphenol Corporation (APH) on February 13, 2026, signaling institutional confidence[1]. Xtrackers Russell US Multifactor ETF also purchased 150 shares, while older filings note some sales by other funds like Longbow Finance SA and Cookson Peirce & Co[2][4][6]. APH stock opened at $143.81 with strong fundamentals including recent earnings beats and positive analyst ratings reiterated in prior weeks[1][2].

No news articles from the last 24 hours (since February 12, 2026, 4 PM UTC) are available in the search results. The most recent mentions include a 'Moderate Buy' analyst consensus from approximately 32-34 minutes before the query time on October 24, 2025, and a stock price gain of 1.33% on July 25, 2025, with positive technical signals like rising volume and buy signals from MACD.

Analyst consensus for AS (Amer Sports Inc) remains positive with a Buy rating from 14 analysts, including 50% Strong Buy and 43% Buy, and a price target of $46.29 as of February 13, 2026.[3] No specific company news, earnings reports, or negative developments for AS appear in the last 24 hours' coverage.[1][2][4][5][6][7][8] Broader market sentiment shows mixed tones with US stocks higher early in the day amid earnings anticipation, while tech faces pressure.[2][7]

Albertsons Companies (ACI) announced the pricing of $2.1 billion in senior notes to refinance existing debt, signaling ongoing financial management efforts[6]. Technical indicators show sell signals from moving averages, with the stock recently oversold on RSI14 and exhibiting negative short-term forecasts[1]. Analyst consensus leans toward Buy from 12 analysts, though options market activity suggests high volatility with no recent earnings estimate upgrades[2][5].

The ASX 200 is set to open lower by 0.6% following US market declines, with oil and gold prices sinking, pressuring energy and gold stocks[2]. Austal shares plunged 22.8% after cutting earnings guidance due to an accounting error on a US Navy contract[4][5]. Positive notes include Bell Potter's buy rating on Cogstate with 34% upside potential and a reaffirmed buy on Temple & Webster[1][2].

Kanzhun Limited (BZ) announced on February 11, 2026, the repurchase of 650,180 shares for over RMB40 million on February 9-10, continuing its 2026 buyback program totaling nearly RMB260 million. On February 12, 2026, the company's Post-IPO Share Scheme trustee purchased 343,304 Class A ordinary shares at an average of US$8.73 per share for employee awards. A February 2026 analysis rates BZ as a Strong Buy with a 75.7/100 composite score, highlighting strong fundamentals including 20% revenue growth and superior margins.

B stock (Barrick Gold Corp) is trading at $44.19 in after-hours sessions, down 3.75% from the regular market close of $45.91.[1] The stock has experienced recent declines with limited positive catalysts reported in available market news.

Amkor Technology (AMKR) stock dropped 8.2% on Friday after the Kim family, its largest shareholder, sold 10 million shares at $48.75 each in a secondary offering, causing a 6.8% premarket decline.[1][3][7] Despite the sell-off, recent analyst actions are positive, with JPMorgan raising its price target to $65 (overweight), Goldman Sachs to $43 (neutral), and Morgan Stanley to $45 (equal weight), alongside a recent earnings beat.[1][2] The options market shows high implied volatility suggesting potential upside, while institutional holdings like Thrivent's $1.69M position persist amid mixed ratings.[2][4]

Corebridge Financial announced a $750 million share repurchase from parent company AIG at $30.42 per share, expected to close on February 17, 2026[1]. The company also increased its quarterly dividend to $0.25[4]. Q4 2025 earnings showed revenue of $6.34 billion (26.4% year-over-year growth) and beat consensus estimates by 25.3%[2].

Fluor (FLR) announced a partnership with Centrus Energy to serve as EPC contractor for uranium enrichment expansion in Ohio, driving premarket gains amid rising demand for low-enriched uranium. Upcoming Q2 earnings on February 17, 2026, show lowered EPS estimates but a value-oriented P/E ratio, with analysts maintaining a consensus Buy rating and $49.57 average price target. Positive backlog growth and nuclear capacity projects support long-term optimism, though class action lawsuits introduce some uncertainty.
